_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Knowledge of analytic standards and frameworks
reference_document_element_description: Knowledge of model evaluation frameworks, including train/validation/test split design, cross-validation, evaluation metric selection, and the conditions under which each metric is appropriate.
comments: NICE knowledge intersects with WIDAI RM domain. Knowledge RM-K-002 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: DQ-T-004 (DQ, cosine: 0.504), AB-K-003 (AB, cosine: 0.497).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark. Both texts processed jointly through transformer; outputs calibrated 0-1 similarity score.
primary_method.significance: STS 6.0/10 (raw 0.597). Shared vocabulary: [frameworks, knowledge]. NICE-only terms [analytic, standards] vs WIDAI-only terms [appropriate, conditions, cross, design, evaluation, metric] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.description: Token-level contextual embedding matching. Precision = FDE coverage of KSA tokens. Recall = KSA coverage by FDE tokens. F1 = harmonic mean.
bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.882 (P=0.925, R=0.843). P exceeds R by 0.083 — the NICE element's 6 words are well-captured by the WIDAI KSA's 22 words, but the KSA contains additional specificity not reflected in the NICE element.

nli_cross_encoder.description: Natural Language Inference. Classifies text pair as entailment, neutral, or contradiction. Indicates directional implication strength.
nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (99.9%). "Knowledge of analytic standards and frameworks" and "Knowledge of model evaluation frameworks, including train/validation/test split" are related but neither implies nor contradicts the other. Standard result for cross-framework mapping where two independent frameworks describe overlapping competencies with independent vocabulary.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity. Each text encoded independently; measures vector-space proximity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.515 < STS 0.597 (gap -0.082). Cross-attention finds deeper alignment than independent encoding. The relationship depends on cross-text reasoning — concept-level mapping between [analytic, standards] and [appropriate, conditions, cross] that bi-encoders structurally cannot capture.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens). Purely lexical baseline, hand-traceable.
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 16.7%. Shared tokens: [frameworks, knowledge]. NICE-only: [analytic, standards]. WIDAI-only: [appropriate, conditions, cross, design, evaluation, metric].
